steps.title: Fastest path: mark the angle relationships first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Use the triangle angle sum property
steps.content:
The sum of the three angles in any triangle is always $180^{\circ}$. Since we know $\angle R = 31^{\circ}$ and $\angle S = 87^{\circ}$, we can write:
$$\angle R + \angle S + \angle T = 180^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Substitute known values and solve for ∠T
steps.content:
Substitute the known angle measures:
$$31^{\circ} + 87^{\circ} + \angle T = 180^{\circ}$$
Combine the known angles:
$$118^{\circ} + \angle T = 180^{\circ}$$
Subtract $118^{\circ}$ from both sides:
$$\angle T = 180^{\circ} - 118^{\circ} = 62^{\circ}$$
That is the correct answer.
